concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes connected by edges.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: An individual element within a graph.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two nodes in a graph.

concepts.name: Unweighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have associated costs or weights.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of nodes connected by edges.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: A path between two nodes with the minimum number of edges.

concepts.name: Source Node
concepts.short_definition: The starting node for finding a shortest path.

concepts.name: Destination Node
concepts.short_definition: The target node for finding a shortest path.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors level by level.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A First-In, First-Out (FIFO) data structure.

concepts.name: Visited Nodes
concepts.short_definition: Nodes that have already been processed by a traversal algorithm.

concepts.name: Distance
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges in a path.

concepts.name: Minimum Distance
concepts.short_definition: The smallest possible distance between two nodes.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each node has a list of its neighbors.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate connections.

concepts.name: Level
concepts.short_definition: The distance of a node from the source node in a BFS traversal.

concepts.name: Parent Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer from a node to the node that discovered it in a traversal.

concepts.name: Path Reconstruction
concepts.short_definition: The process of finding the actual shortest path using parent pointers.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Subgraphs where any two nodes are connected to each other by paths.

concepts.name: Disconnected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph that is not connected.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path that starts and ends at the same node.

concepts.name: Acyclic Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph that contains no cycles.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected acyclic graph.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree structure.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node with no children in a tree structure.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a graph.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A way of organizing and storing data.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the runtime of an algorithm scales with input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the memory usage of an algorithm scales with input size.

concepts.name: Edge Case
concepts.short_definition: A specific scenario that might cause an algorithm to fail or behave unexpectedly.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Different ways to store and represent graph data.

concepts.name: Reachability
concepts.short_definition: The ability to reach one node from another through a path.

concepts.name: Unreachable Nodes
concepts.short_definition: Nodes that cannot be reached from a given source node.

concepts.name: Shortest Path Problem
concepts.short_definition: The problem of finding the shortest path between two nodes in a graph.

concepts.name: Single-Source Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding the shortest paths from a single source node to all other nodes.

concepts.name: All-Pairs Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding the shortest paths between every pair of nodes in a graph.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: The specific implementation of BFS for finding shortest paths in unweighted graphs.
